jwcListedAreas.latestUpdate: Added: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Gulf of Oman — high-risk classification after the de facto closure of Hormuz in early March. No areas have been removed since.

editorialReading: Premiums have hardened rather than continued to spike — the structural risk floor is forming. The JWC has not removed any areas from the high-risk list since the Gulf cluster was added on 3 March, and underwriters have begun pricing two layers (transit and facility) in their Middle East book. The historical reference is the 1980s tanker war: Lloyd's premiums for Gulf transit remained elevated above pre-conflict baselines for most of the decade after the 1988 ceasefire.
watchNext: The next JWC review meeting is scheduled for mid-June. Any removal of an area from the list — particularly any partial de-listing of Persian Gulf transit — would signal that underwriters are pricing the conflict as transient rather than structural. No removal would confirm the regime-change reading.
methodologyNote: Premium ranges aggregated from publicly-cited figures in news sources; exact rates are confidential between brokers and underwriters. JWC Listed Areas from Lloyd's Joint War Committee. Editorial reading is our market interpretation, not a republished source.
